planned_actions: Step 1: cd src/cli && npm run build (tsc compile to dist/) — verify exit 0.
Step 2: cd src/cli && npm run bundle (esbuild → plugin/bin/geas) — verify exit 0 and that plugin/bin/geas mtime is refreshed.

Step 3: Bundle internal string scan — since esbuild emits a CJS JS file (with shebang banner), use grep/rg directly on plugin/bin/geas (not native `strings`). Pattern: search for '--data ' and '--file ' occurrences and .option('--data', .option('--file' declarations. Classify each hit; assert only whitelisted survivors remain (event log --data, context write --data, packet create --file/--content). Baseline 15 → expect large drop.

Step 4: Repo-wide grep guard — run Grep across src/cli/, plugin/skills/, plugin/agents/, src/cli/test/ for '--data ' and '--file '. Exclude whitelist (event.ts, context.ts, packet.ts, runtime .geas/missions/** self-references). Any JSON-intent hit is a fail.
Step 5: Smoke test form 1 (pipe) — `printf '{"id":"smoke-t003","title":"t"}' | ./plugin/bin/geas mission write-spec` inside a scratch dir; assert exit 0 and spec.json written.
Step 6: Smoke test form 2 (heredoc) — `./plugin/bin/geas task create <<'EOF' ... EOF` with a valid task JSON; assert exit 0.
Step 7: Smoke test form 3 (redirection) — write evidence JSON to a temp file, then `./plugin/bin/geas evidence add ... < /tmp/ev.json`; assert exit 0.

Step 8: TTY-hang simulation — node one-liner using child_process.spawnSync with stdio ['inherit','pipe','pipe'] and timeout 1000ms against `./plugin/bin/geas mission write-spec`; assert process exits non-zero within the timeout (no hang, no ETIMEDOUT kill-by-signal on a raw TTY read).
Step 9: cd src/cli && npm test — run full test suite; expect exit 0.
Step 10: Collect evidence artifacts (build log, bundle hash, grep counts, smoke outputs, tty exit code) into the reviewer packet.
edge_cases: plugin/bin/geas is a bundled JS file with a `#!/usr/bin/env node` banner, NOT a native ELF/PE binary. Windows `strings.exe` would work but is unnecessary — grep/rg on the text is sufficient and more precise. Do not invoke `strings`.

Whitelist survivors in bundle: `event log --data`, `context write --data`, `packet create --file`, `packet create --content`. These are JSON-preservation or file-copy use cases, not JSON-input-as-string. Match exact option strings to avoid false positives from generated commander help text.

Heredoc smoke test quoting: use `<<'EOF'` (single-quoted delimiter) to prevent bash from expanding `$`, backticks, or backslashes inside the JSON body. Double-quoted heredoc would corrupt JSON containing `$`.

TTY simulation on Windows: spawnSync with `stdin: 'inherit'` inherits the parent's stdin (which under node test runner is typically a pipe, not a real TTY). Use `{ stdio: ['inherit', 'pipe', 'pipe'], timeout: 1000 }` and assert `result.status !== 0 && result.signal !== 'SIGTERM'` — meaning the CLI detected missing stdin and exited non-zero on its own, not because the timeout killed it. If signal is set, the hang guard failed.

Bundle regeneration must actually overwrite plugin/bin/geas — stat mtime before/after as a sanity check. If git shows no diff but content changed, that's fine (reproducible build); if mtime unchanged, bundle silently no-op'd.
Smoke tests must run in a throwaway project directory (mktemp) so they don't pollute A:/geas/.geas runtime state.
Grep for '--data ' with trailing space to avoid matching '--data-source' or similar; same for '--file '.
state_transitions: plugin/bin/geas: regenerated content (stale task-001 build → fresh stdin-first CLI). File path unchanged, shebang preserved, executable bit preserved on POSIX.
No source code changes: src/cli/**, plugin/skills/**, plugin/agents/** untouched.
No .geas/ runtime writes except this contract and subsequent evidence/events via CLI.
non_goals: Do NOT modify any CLI source under src/cli/ — task-001 is the source-of-truth for stdin behavior.
Do NOT edit skill or agent docs — task-002 already swept them.
Do NOT modify src/cli/esbuild.config.mjs unless the build fails and requires a config fix (escalate first).
Do NOT add new tests — use the existing test suite via `npm test`.
Do NOT commit; closure packet assembly and verdict happen in downstream phases.
demo_steps: AC1 — `cd src/cli && npm run build` → exit 0, dist/ refreshed.
AC2 — `cd src/cli && npm run bundle` → exit 0, plugin/bin/geas updated (mtime refreshed).
AC3 — grep plugin/bin/geas for '--data '/'--file ' option declarations → only whitelisted hits (event/context/packet); baseline 15 → significant reduction.
AC4 — repo-wide grep across src/cli/, plugin/skills/, plugin/agents/, src/cli/test/ → 0 non-whitelisted JSON-input hits.
AC5 — `cd src/cli && npm test` → all suites pass.
AC6 — three smoke tests (pipe, heredoc, redirection) against mission write-spec / task create / evidence add → all exit 0 with expected artifacts.
AC7 — node spawnSync TTY simulation with 1s timeout → non-zero exit without timeout-kill signal, confirming no hang.

known_risks: TTY simulation on Windows requires process.execPath wrapper; shebang invocation via spawnSync does not work direct. Noted for downstream tooling.
untested_paths: macOS/Linux shebang execution path — bundled binary is JS-with-shebang, tested only on Windows (Git Bash); CI may surface differences
self_check.summary: Bundle regenerated, mtime bumped, 31/31 tests pass. Bundled grep shows 4 residuals, all whitelisted (event log --data, context write --data, packet create --file/--content help). Repo-wide grep: 23 --data (all event log), 0 --file. Smoke tested pipe/heredoc/redirection forms — no hangs. TTY sim exits non-zero with NO_STDIN stderr, no SIGTERM. 7/7 ACs pass.

tier_1.details: closure_ready profile: skip mechanical per spec

tier_2.details: 7/7 ACs met: build+bundle green, 31/31 tests, bundled grep whitelist-only, repo grep 0 violations, 3 stdin forms work, TTY hang guard verified

closure.change_summary: plugin/bin/geas 리번들(esbuild CJS 산출) 및 전수 검증. 번들 내부 grep: 4개 --data/--file 문자열 전부 화이트리스트(event log, context write, packet create --file/--content, help text) — 베이스라인 15에서 감소. 레포 전수 grep: 23개 event log --data 잔존, JSON 입력 용례 0건. 31/31 tests pass. 3-form stdin 스모크(pipe/heredoc/redirection) 무-hang 확인. TTY 시뮬레이션에서 non-zero exit + NO_STDIN stderr, SIGTERM 미발생.

reviews.summary: Bundle verification strategy sound; 3 minor advisory concerns

open_risks: Windows shebang wrapping: spawnSync on plugin/bin/geas needs process.execPath; not an issue for direct shell invocation
POSIX shebang execution path (macOS/Linux) not yet exercised in CI

verdict.rationale: Closure packet complete. All 3 reviewers approved (platform-engineer, qa-engineer, design-authority). Self-check confidence 5/5. Gate pass across Tier 0/2 (Tier 1 skipped per closure_ready profile). 7/7 ACs independently verified: build+bundle green, 31/31 tests, bundled grep shows only whitelisted --data/--file survivors (down from baseline 15), repo-wide grep 0 JSON-input violations, 3-form stdin smoke tests (pipe/heredoc/redirection) pass without hang, TTY simulation exits non-zero with NO_STDIN stderr and no SIGTERM. Open risks (Windows-only TTY verification, POSIX shebang not yet exercised in CI) are documented, non-blocking, and appropriate for a CLI rebundle task — product value delivered: stale bundled binary now reflects task-001 stdin-first CLI. Ship.

what_went_well: Bundle regenerated cleanly, 31/31 tests green, 7/7 ACs verified on first pass
Grep whitelist approach (event log/context write/packet create as allowed non-JSON flags) provided a mechanical, unambiguous success signal
TTY hang simulation verified the NO_STDIN guard end-to-end against the bundled binary
No rework; task-001 + task-002 upstream work integrated cleanly
what_was_surprising: Windows spawnSync cannot execute plugin/bin/geas via shebang directly; requires process.execPath wrapper
Bundled JS grep surfaced 4 residuals (baseline 15); one was help text, explicit understanding narrowed what qualifies as an option-declaration hit
rule_candidates: For CLI grep verification, distinguish option-declaration strings (commander .option() output) from help text mentions; both appear as residuals but have different semantic weight
memory_candidates: platform-engineer: On Windows, spawnSync against plugin/bin/geas must use process.execPath as argv[0] and the script path as argv[1]; shebang routing is shell-specific (Git Bash works, raw spawn does not)
debt_candidates: POSIX shebang execution smoke test for plugin/bin/geas on macOS/Linux
next_time_guidance: When writing bundle-verification tasks, include both a declaration-only grep and a help-text-aware grep to pre-classify residuals
Cross-platform spawn of the bundled CLI should standardize on node + script-path wrapping
